boundaries.reason: Proves an explicitly owned opaque host value can cross the semantic audit.
Proves an explicitly indexable opaque host record can be allowlisted without opening ordinary interfaces.
DynamicAccess<Unknown> intentionally models an explicitly indexable foreign object; ordinary interfaces remain closed.
BoundaryTypes deliberately exercises reusable unknown narrowing helpers and returns typed values immediately after guards.
Recursive JSON objects are intentionally string-indexed while every value remains within the closed JsonValue algebra.
JsonAlias deliberately carries the recursive JsonValue object boundary through its typed passthrough signature.
Haxe class names are runtime strings, so this reflection registry needs string lookup while every stored value remains a Function.
Haxe enum names are runtime strings, so this reflection registry needs string lookup while each value keeps the small enum metadata shape used by the runtime.
The shared runtime registry can store classes, enums, and internal helpers under names discovered at runtime. Values remain unknown until a typed registry view validates their role.

Register contains the one low-level bridge for Haxe's dynamic JavaScript runtime: reflection dictionaries, iterator discovery, deferred superclass constructors, safe assertions, and bound-method caches. Each escape is documented beside its Haxe implementation and ordinary generated modules regain precise types immediately.
A JSON object accepts arbitrary string property names, but each property value stays inside the closed recursive JsonValue type instead of becoming any or unknown.

JsonCodec accepts an unknown host value only at its validation entry points, then returns the closed recursive JsonValue type. JSON objects remain string-indexed because JSON property names are not known in advance.
UnknownNarrow deliberately receives opaque host values and exposes only guarded conversions. Callers receive a concrete type or null after the runtime check.
Haxe class names are runtime strings, so this reflection registry needs string lookup while every stored value remains a Function.
Haxe enum names are runtime strings, so this reflection registry needs string lookup while each value keeps the small enum metadata shape used by the runtime.
The shared runtime registry can store classes, enums, and internal helpers under names discovered at runtime. Values remain unknown until a typed registry view validates their role.

Register contains the one low-level bridge for Haxe's dynamic JavaScript runtime: reflection dictionaries, iterator discovery, deferred superclass constructors, safe assertions, and bound-method caches. Each escape is documented beside its Haxe implementation and ordinary generated modules regain precise types immediately.
A JSON object accepts arbitrary string property names, but each property value stays inside the closed recursive JsonValue type instead of becoming any or unknown.
JsonArray can contain recursive JSON objects; those objects use string keys while all nested values remain inside JsonValue.
JsonNonNullValue can still be a JSON object, whose property names are arbitrary strings but whose values stay inside the JSON algebra.
JSON object property names are not known in advance, so JsonObject is string-indexed while its values remain precisely JsonValue.
JsonValue is a closed recursive union that includes string-keyed JSON objects; the index does not permit values outside that union.
The declaration module repeats JsonArray so consumers can name its recursive JSON shape; nested objects remain string-indexed and typed as JsonValue.

JsonCodec accepts an unknown host value only at its validation entry points, then returns the closed recursive JsonValue type. JSON objects remain string-indexed because JSON property names are not known in advance.
The declaration module repeats JsonNonNullValue so consumers can name its recursive JSON shape; nested object keys remain strings and values remain typed.
The declaration module repeats JsonObject for consumers; arbitrary string keys are valid JSON while every value stays inside JsonValue.
The declaration module repeats the closed JsonValue union; its string index comes only from the JSON object member of that union.
UnknownNarrow deliberately receives opaque host values and exposes only guarded conversions. Callers receive a concrete type or null after the runtime check.
